evals.prompt: Test short conversation (5 messages) with topic_id strategy. Create 5 messages about Python file editing, run prepare_messages(), measure token usage.
evals.expected_output: Token usage, message count, strategy working

evals.prompt: Test sliding_window strategy with same 5 messages. Compare token usage to topic_id.
Run conversation with 20 messages (mixed lengths) using topic_id strategy. Measure retention rate.
Test all 5 strategies with identical 20-message conversation. Aggregate results to benchmark.json
evals.expected_output: Benchmark with token usage for all strategies

evals.prompt: Test topic cache has() method with fake topic ID. Verify returns False (hallucination prevention).
Test topic cache get_content() with valid ID. Verify returns stored content.
Run 50-message conversation approaching context limit with topic_id. Measure usage_percent.
Compare summary vs topic_id for 30-message conversation. Which preserves more detail?
Test concurrent writes to topic cache. 10 threads writing 50 topics each.
Create benchmark.csv with all 5 strategies: short/medium/long conversations, token usage, retention %
evals.expected_output: CSV file with 15 rows (5 strategies x 3 lengths)

evals.prompt: Run forced compaction test: set max_tokens=500,run with 100 messages. Compare sliding_window vs topic_id output counts.
evals.expected_output: sliding_window drops older, topic_id keeps more (until LLM extraction)

evals.prompt: Verify ContextStrategy.TOPIC_ID enum exists and equals 'topic_id'.
Test topic cache with SHA256 hash: same content produces same ID, different produces different.
Test corrupt JSON handling: write bad JSON to topic file, then call get(). Should return None.
evals.expected_output: Returns None for corrupt file (no crash)

evals.prompt: Test empty content filtering: call put('', 'fact') and put('   ', 'fact'). Should not create valid topic IDs.
evals.expected_output: Empty content filtered, no topic created

evals.prompt: Run all strategies with 200 messages, measure which preserves most user content vs assistant content separately.
evals.expected_output: Preservation rates for user vs assistant messages

evals.prompt: Test topic_id strategy with LLM present but no messages to extract. Should handle gracefully.
Create stress test: 1000 messages with topic_id strategy, measure memory and performance.
Verify topic IDs follow format topic_8hex (e.g., topic_a1b2c3d4). Test with 100 different contents.
Generate final comparison report: strategy recommendations for short/medium/long conversations.
